steps.title: Find the main claim
steps.content: The text contrasts private (republican-era) and public (imperial-era) Roman libraries, then pivots with however to Fernandes's view: "these two traditions aren't as distinct as they seem" because both functioned as extensions of personal patronage.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content:
The whole passage builds toward that thesis — that private and public Roman libraries shared an essential similarity (both were tools of patronage).

  • A focuses only on republican access
  • B argues a value judgment never made
  • D is about disappearance — none capture the central similarity claim
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
